title: tmuxcc data-plane binary frame format
description:
Documentation schema for the data-plane binary frame format. The data plane carries raw terminal output bytes for panes. This is NOT a JSON Schema for validation — binary frames are not JSON and cannot be validated with ajv. This file documents the layout for language-neutral implementations.

Frame layout (every frame is a contiguous byte sequence):

  Offset  Size  Field
  ------  ----  -------------------------------------------------------
       0     1  MAGIC  — 0xCC (mnemonic: control-client)
       1     4  SEQ    — uint32 big-endian; per-pane monotonic counter
       5     4  PAYLEN — uint32 big-endian; payload byte length
       9     2  IDLEN  — uint16 big-endian; paneId UTF-8 byte length
      11  IDLEN  PANEID — paneId encoded as UTF-8 bytes (variable length)
  11+IDLEN PAYLEN PAYLOAD — raw terminal output bytes (opaque; any byte value)

Constants:
  FRAME_MAGIC = 0xCC
  MAX_FRAME   = 8,388,608 bytes (8 MiB)

Sequence numbers:
  SEQ is a per-pane monotonically-increasing uint32 starting at 0.
  The session-proxy owns the counter for each pane and increments per frame.
  Clients use SEQ to detect frame drops and restore ordering.
  Wrapping at 0xFFFFFFFF is allowed.

Decoding:
  1. Read 11 bytes (MIN_HEADER_BYTES).
  2. Verify buf[0] === 0xCC; throw RangeError on mismatch.
  3. Read PAYLEN (uint32 BE at offset 5); reject if > MAX_FRAME.
  4. Read IDLEN (uint16 BE at offset 9).
  5. Total frame length = 11 + IDLEN + PAYLEN.
  6. Wait until all bytes are buffered; emit DataFrame {paneId, seq, payload}.

Why binary, not JSON/base64?
  A busy terminal can push megabytes per second. JSON encoding adds per-byte
  string-escape overhead; base64 inflates every payload by 33%.
  The control plane (JSON) is low-volume and benefits from human-readability;
  the data plane does not.

DataFrame.description: Logical representation of a decoded data-plane frame. This JSON shape is used in golden transcripts and tests to describe frame content — it is NOT the binary wire format.

paneId.description: Which pane's output stream this frame belongs to.

payloadHex.description: Payload bytes encoded as a lowercase hex string (2 hex chars per byte). The actual wire payload is raw binary; hex is used here for JSON representability.
